title: Wholesome Turkish Almonds Kunefe
description: A resinous, aerated hangover cure Turkish dessert featuring almond extract, bhuna roasted almonds, and a lotus leaf wrap.
instructions: Bring 200ml water to a boil in a saucepan, then add 200g fine semolina, reduce heat, and cook for 8 minutes, stirring frequently until thickened, then set aside to cool.
Melt 50g unsalted butter and 20g ghee in a skillet over medium heat, stir in 80g sugar and 30g honey until dissolved, and remove from heat.
Combine the butter-ghee syrup with the warm semolina, fold in 1 tsp almond extract and a pinch of salt, and let the mixture cool to room temperature.
In a dry pan, bhuna 100g ground almonds over low heat until fragrant and lightly toasted, about 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Fold the toasted almonds into the semolina mixture, ensuring even distribution.
Line a lotus leaf wrap with parchment paper, pour a layer of the semolina–almond mixture onto the center, and press gently to form a 5cm square.
Drizzle 1 tbsp fresh lemon juice over the top, and sprinkle a light dusting of powdered sugar for garnish.
Fold the lotus leaf sides over the filling, sealing them with a small stick of butter, making a compact roll.
Place the wrapped roll in a preheated oven at 180°C for 20 minutes until the exterior is slightly golden.
Remove from oven, let cool 5 minutes, then cut into bite-sized pieces and serve warm or at room temperature.
